repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: In `vim.pack.update()` confirmation buffer it might be useful to be able to use `gx` (open link at cursor) when cursor is on something like commit or tag.

Solution: Add `textDocument/documentLink` method support for the in-process LSP. This may be used by LSP clients and makes `gx` automatically work.

The shortcoming is that this requires tracking how to construct a URL from source and commit/tag. ~~Currently only GitHub hosted repositories are supported.~~ Special links are computed via a best effort educated guess about their structure.

---

Here is an example of which parts of confirmation buffer are reported as links (enclosed in `!`):

```
# Update ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## nvim-lspconfig
Path:            !/home/user/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/core/opt/nvim-lspconfig!
Source:          !https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig!
Revision before: !766ebf84062f8f96e698e7ed47adebe8d60a32e6!
Revision after:  !81878de76c0de4ce289513734ad80c31ec7871b8! (master)

Pending updates:
> !81878de! │ docs: update configs.md skip-checks: true
> !6db8f68! │ docs: update generated annotations skip-checks: true

# Same ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## mini.nvim
Path:     !/home/user/.local/share/nvim/site/pack/core/opt/mini.nvim!
Source:   !https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim!
Revision: !94cae4660a8b2d95dbbd56e1fbc6fcfa2716d152! (v0.16.0)

Available newer versions:
• !v0.17.0!
```

---

Resolve #38700

I am not sure if this type of addition is backported. It would make future backports easier, but this is closer to a new feature than a fix.

comment_data.body:
what's an example of what it returns?

```suggestion
--- @return string? # Example: "..."
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -129,4 +129,41 @@ function M.term_exitcode()
   return ''
 end
 
+--- Compute a link to a target on a forge host
+--- @param repo string URL of repo, usually "https://<domain>/<user>/<name>"
+--- @param target string Identifier of a target, like commit hash or tag name
+--- @param target_type "commit"|"tag"
+--- @return string?

comment_data.body: nit/nonblocker, `get_forge_url` is more inline with our existing names

   return ''
 end
 
+--- Compute a link to a target on a forge host

+--- @param target string Identifier of a target, like commit hash or tag name
+--- @param target_type "commit"|"tag"
+--- @return string?
+function M.compute_forge_link(repo, target, target_type)

comment_data.body:
what is an example of what the input this expects? that adds only 1 line to the docstring and immediately makes the function understandable.

    --- Compares things like "..."
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -64,6 +73,72 @@ end
 
 --- @alias vim.pack.lsp.Position { line: integer, character: integer }
 --- @alias vim.pack.lsp.Range { start: vim.pack.lsp.Position, end: vim.pack.lsp.Position }

+

+--- @param pattern string
+--- @param link_type string
+--- @param lnum number
+--- @param src string
+--- @return vim.pack.lsp.DocumentLink?
+local function match_link(line, pattern, link_type, lnum, src)

comment_data.body: There is now a `# Example: <repo>/releases/tag/<target>`. Is this enough or should this be a full (rather wide) URL?

comment_data.body: I've added a full description to all parameters and the title (as concise as I was able to come up with).

comment_regular_data.body:
> Currently only GitHub hosted repositories are supported.

and this is already a fair bit of code. WDYT about extracting it to an internal module, just to make it clear that it's already orthogonal to vim.pack

> > Currently only GitHub hosted repositories are supported.
> 
> and this is already a fair bit of code. WDYT about extracting it to an internal module, just to make it clear that it's already orthogonal to vim.pack

The actual computation of a URL is only [three lines of code](https://github.com/echasnovski/neovim/blob/ef4851b10a3066c7c3c8e209cd6fd006f3d776d6/runtime/lua/vim/pack/_lsp.lua#L108-L110). Everything else is concerned with parsing confirmation buffer to find relevant link locations and creating a proper LSP method.

Do you suggest to extract those lines relevant to only link creation? I am not quite opposed, but not sure if that is worth it. If you'd still prefer that, I'd need specific suggestions where and how you'd want that to look.
It seems like this functionality of leveraging LSP for plugins is worth having as a separate module? Like what #24338 tries to do.

> > Currently only GitHub hosted repositories are supported.
> 
> and this is already a fair bit of code. WDYT about extracting it to an internal module, just to make it clear that it's already orthogonal to vim.pack

@justinmk, I pushed a separate commit that adds `require('vim._core.util').compute_forge_link()` with a much more popular public Git forge coverage. Tested for both commits and tags based on these plugins:
- https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig
- https://codeberg.org/mfussenegger/nvim-dap (computes link via a Forgejo fallback)
- https://git.sr.ht/~whynothugo/lsp_lines.nvim
- https://tangled.org/olexsmir.xyz/gopher.nvim (doesn't work with short commit hash, but that might change in the future)
- https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/editor-extensions/gitlab.vim

title: feat(pack): support `textDocument/documentLink` in confirmation buffer
